reason: Enabling FIPS mode requires adding the kernel boot parameter fips=1 and changing the system-wide cryptographic policy. On an already installed RHEL 9 system this is unsupported and may render the system unbootable, causing a permanent lockout. The policy explicitly categorizes FIPS changes as too dangerous to remediate automatically.
estimated_impact: reboot required; Potential system unbootable after kernel parameter change; Cryptographic policy alteration may break services; Unsupported on existing installations
